intro: Helbah (related in meaning to Helbon, meaning milk; fatness) was a town in the territory of Asher from which the tribe failed to expel the Canaanite inhabitants (Judg. 1:31). It appears in the list of unoccupied Canaanite towns alongside Achzib, Helbah, Aphik, and Rehob, reflecting the broader pattern of incomplete conquest in the coastal and northern regions. The site has not been positively identified.
